ignore-generated-files.label: Keep Generated Files Out of History
ignore-generated-files.summary: Add exact ignore rules and remove generated output from future tracked snapshots.

sketch2.title: Tracked Files Need an Extra Step

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.title: Add the Exact Ignore Rules
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.prompt:
In the Explorer, create `community-site/.gitignore`. Add exactly these two lines starting on line 1:

`dist/`
`*.log`

Save it. Then type `touch debug.log` and press Enter. Type `touch dist/preview.js` and press Enter. Finally, type `git status --short --ignored` and press Enter.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.hint: Create .gitignore with exactly two lines, then create the two supplied local-output paths.

help.concept: .gitignore describes untracked paths Git should normally leave out of status and staging.
help.hint_1: Use exactly dist/ and *.log on separate lines.
help.hint_2: The generated preview and log should appear as ignored in the requested status view.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.solutionCode:
touch debug.log
touch dist/preview.js
git status --short --ignored
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.instructions:
In the Explorer, create `community-site/.gitignore`. Add exactly these two lines starting on line 1:

`dist/`
`*.log`

Save it. Then type `touch debug.log` and press Enter. Type `touch dist/preview.js` and press Enter. Finally, type `git status --short --ignored` and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html rm -f -- community-site/notes/old-events.txt rmdir -- community-site/notes 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Organize community files'

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Organize community files'

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.title: Stop Tracking the Legacy Preview
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.prompt: `dist/legacy-preview.js` was tracked before the ignore rule existed. Type `git add .gitignore` and press Enter. Then type `git rm --cached dist/legacy-preview.js` and press Enter. The file should remain in the working directory but leave the next tracked snapshot.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.hint: Use --cached so Git stops tracking the path without deleting the working copy.

help.concept: Ignore rules do not automatically untrack a file already in history.
help.hint_1: The file should still exist after the command.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.instructions: `dist/legacy-preview.js` was tracked before the ignore rule existed. Type `git add .gitignore` and press Enter. Then type `git rm --cached dist/legacy-preview.js` and press Enter. The file should remain in the working directory but leave the next tracked snapshot.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.title: Commit the Ignore Policy
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.prompt: The exact ignore file and tracked-file removal are staged. Type `git commit -m "Ignore generated output"` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.hint: Record the ignore policy and tracked-file cleanup together.

help.concept: The commit records the policy and removes generated output from future tracked snapshots.
help.hint_2: Ignored files may remain on disk while short status stays clean.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Ignore generated output"
git status --short
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.instructions: The exact ignore file and tracked-file removal are staged. Type `git commit -m "Ignore generated output"` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you run `git commit -m "Ignore generated output"`?

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.title: Purpose of .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a `.gitignore` file in a Git repository?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To track changes in all files
options.b: To ignore specific files and directories
options.c: To delete files from the repository
options.d: To rename files in the repository

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.title: Effect of Adding to .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.prompt: What happens when you add a file to `.gitignore` that has already been committed?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is automatically deleted from the repository
options.b: The file is untracked and removed from history
options.c: The file remains tracked and in history
options.d: The file is renamed in the repository

ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.title: Useful Ignore Patterns
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.prompt: Which patterns match generated or local-only files used in this course? Select all that apply.
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.title: Stop Tracking a Local-Only File
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.prompt: Arrange the safe steps for a file that is already tracked but should remain only on each developer’s machine.
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t2: Remove the path from Git’s index while keeping the working copy

ignore-generated-files-sketch0.title: Ignore Rules Describe Untracked Noise
ignore-generated-files-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: Generated previews and logs can be recreated. `.gitignore` keeps matching untracked paths out of normal staging and status while leaving useful source files visible.

ignore-generated-files-sketch2.title: Tracked Files Need an Extra Step
ignore-generated-files-sketch2.bodyMarkdown: A new ignore rule does not remove a path already tracked. `git rm --cached path` removes it from the proposed tracked snapshot while keeping the working copy.

ignore-generated-files-sketch3.title: The Policy Is Project Source
ignore-generated-files-sketch3.bodyMarkdown: Commit `.gitignore` with the tracked-file cleanup. The generated files remain on disk and ignored, while the repository ends clean.
